outcome_contract.why: Staff and operators have no way to tell whether the AI email drafts are getting better or worse over time. They cannot see how long it takes to resolve a guest enquiry, how many drafts pass quality checks, or whether email volume is trending up. Without this visibility, problems go unnoticed until a guest complains.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: A validated next action exists for inbox analytics, with required metrics, computation approach, and UI surface identified.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: InboxWorkspace shows basic counts (threads, manual draft, ready-to-send) but no analytics panel. No charts or summary views exist.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No analytics UI exists. Need to decide tab vs. page vs. inline summary.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes -- UI approach is a primary decision

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Draft-stats endpoint returns `insufficient: true` when no events exist.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Need empty states for all new metrics, loading states for analytics queries, error handling for D1 query failures.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: All inbox API routes use `requireStaffAuth`. Draft-stats follows this pattern.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New analytics endpoints must require staff auth. No PII in aggregated metrics.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Thread events are the audit trail. telemetry.server.ts has critical vs best-effort distinction.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Analytics computation errors should be logged. Consider adding an event for analytics access.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Draft-stats endpoint has zero test coverage. Telemetry recording is well-tested.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New analytics code needs unit tests for SQL queries and data transformations.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: D1 schema has thread_events indexed on (thread_id, timestamp) and (timestamp). No event_type index. quality_json stored on draft rows.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: May need new index on event_type for efficient aggregation. API response contract needs definition.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: D1 has per-request compute limits. Draft-stats endpoint runs complex CTEs.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Analytics queries on growing event table could hit D1 limits. Consider query complexity budget.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Schema migrations are sequential (0001-0004). New indexes require a migration.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New migration for index. API endpoints are additive (no breaking changes). UI behind existing InboxWorkspace.

open_operator_questions: What time granularity matters most for trend views -- daily, weekly, or both?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The reception inbox records telemetry events for every step in the email draft lifecycle (admitted, drafted, sent, resolved, dismissed, recovery, etc.) in a D1 SQLite database, but provides no aggregated analytics or reporting surface. Staff and operators cannot see draft quality pass rates, average resolution time, volume trends, or admission rates. A draft-stats endpoint exists but is limited to acceptance rate only and is only exposed via MCP. This fact-find investigates what telemetry data already exists, what metrics are most valuable, how they can be computed, and where the analytics sur
goals: Map the complete set of telemetry events recorded and the data available in each
Identify quality-check data stored alongside drafts
Determine which metrics provide the most operational value
Evaluate computation approaches (D1 SQL aggregations vs. API-computed vs. materialized)
Recommend where the analytics surface should live in the UI
non_goals: Alerting when quality rates drop below thresholds (adjacent-later)
Redesigning the telemetry recording system itself
constraints: Reception uses Cloudflare D1 (SQLite) as its database; no PostgreSQL aggregation features available
D1 has read performance limits; analytics queries must not block the operational inbox
Reception is a Cloudflare Worker with limited compute budget per request
assumptions: Thread volume is low-to-moderate (hostel inbox, not high-volume SaaS)
D1 query performance is sufficient for simple aggregations over the event table at current scale
key_entry_points: `apps/reception/src/lib/inbox/telemetry.server.ts` -- telemetry recording layer; defines all event types and recording functions
`apps/reception/src/components/inbox/InboxWorkspace.tsx` -- inbox UI entry point; currently shows only thread count and manual-draft/ready-to-send counts

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: No event_type index exists; analytics queries filtering by event_type may scan
None -- confirms feasible SQL aggregation pattern for D1
None -- clear extension point (tab or header summary)
None -- existing draft_acceptance_rate demonstrates the pattern
None -- requireStaffAuth on all inbox routes
None -- inbox_recovery events have structured outcome codes
